source.description: Built-in image generation contact sheet reviewed before cropping; approved final panel is saved as the finished raster artwork.
source.notes: All step frames should be cropped from the same generated contact sheet so the hull, cabin, smokestack, portholes, rail, waves, graphite texture, and restrained blue color stay consistent.

process_strategy: Use one six-panel contact sheet that builds the tugboat from simple hull and cabin construction to the side-view boat shape, cabin details, stack, rail, water ripples, restrained color, and final keeper-line strength.

final_elements.name: rounded tugboat hull
final_elements.description: A small side-view tugboat hull with a curved bow and flatter stern.

final_elements.name: cabin block
final_elements.description: A simple boxy cabin sitting on top of the hull.

final_elements.name: cabin roof
final_elements.description: A slightly rounded roof cap above the cabin.

final_elements.name: smokestack
final_elements.description: A short stack rising behind the cabin roof.

final_elements.name: round portholes
final_elements.description: Two or three small round windows along the hull.

final_elements.name: cabin windows
final_elements.description: Small rectangular cabin windows facing forward and sideways.

final_elements.name: deck rail and bumper line
final_elements.description: A short deck rail and a dark bumper stripe following the hull.

final_elements.name: water ripples
final_elements.description: A few loose wave marks under the tugboat.

final_elements.name: restrained blue color
final_elements.description: Light blue colored-pencil touches on the hull, windows, and water.

frames.visible_job: Sets the side-view tugboat hull with a light curved guide and simple cabin placement marks.
Adds the boxy cabin and rounded roof cap on top of the hull.
Clarifies the bow, stern, deck edge, cabin silhouette, and short smokestack.
Adds round portholes along the hull and small cabin windows.
Adds the deck rail, bumper stripe, water ripples, and restrained blue colored-pencil touches.

final_step.visible_job: Only strengthens existing hull, cabin, roof, stack, windows, rail, bumper stripe, waves, color, and shadow marks without adding new boat parts or scenery.
allowed_changes: paper texture from the saved contact sheet

rejection_checks: No hull contour, cabin, roof, stack, porthole, cabin window, rail, bumper stripe, water ripple, or core color choice should first appear in the final image.
The tugboat must keep the same side-view pose and proportions in every frame.
The final image must not add a harbor, sun, passengers, lettering, flags, extra boats, or background scenery absent from the process frames.
Do not use separately prompted step images that drift from this contact sheet.
